38	class TokenDatabase(metaclass=abc.ABCMeta):
39	    """TokenDatabase is used to convert input tokens into list of
40	    cache engine keys. There are multiple ways to implement this:
41	
42	    - ChunkedTokenDatabase: It processes tokens into chunks and convert
43	    each chunk into a cache engine key using prefix hash.
44	
45	    - SegmentTokenDatabase: It processes tokens into segments based on
46	    special separators and convert each segment into a cache engine key.
47	    """
48	
49	    @abc.abstractmethod
50	    def __init__(
51	        self,
52	        config: Optional[LMCacheEngineConfig] = None,
53	        metadata: Optional[LMCacheMetadata] = None,
54	    ):
55	        global NONE_HASH
56	
57	        hash_algorithm: str = (
58	            config.pre_caching_hash_algorithm if config is not None else "builtin"
59	        )
60	
61	        # Get hash function with vLLM version compatibility
62	        self.hash_func = self._get_vllm_hash_func(hash_algorithm)
63	
64	        # Initialize NONE_HASH (vLLM >= PR#20511)
65	        # NOTE: For centralized cache sharing, ensure PYTHONHASHSEED is
66	        # set consistently across all processes (e.g., export PYTHONHASHSEED=0).
67	        try:
68	            # Third Party
69	            from vllm.v1.core import kv_cache_utils
70	
71	            if hasattr(kv_cache_utils, "init_none_hash"):
72	                kv_cache_utils.init_none_hash(self.hash_func)
73	                NONE_HASH = kv_cache_utils.NONE_HASH
74	                logger.info(
75	                    f"Initialized NONE_HASH={NONE_HASH} from vLLM (>= PR#20511)"
76	                )
77	            else:
78	                NONE_HASH = 0
79	                logger.info("Using default NONE_HASH=0 (vLLM < PR#20511)")
80	        except (ImportError, AttributeError):
81	            NONE_HASH = 0
82	            logger.info("Using default NONE_HASH=0 (vLLM not available)")
83	
84	        logger.info(f"Using hash algorithm: {hash_algorithm}")
85	        self.metadata = metadata
86	        # Whether only the first rank should save cache. This flag is also used
87	        # to control the logical world_size embedded into CacheEngineKey.
88	        self.save_only_first_rank = False
89	        if config is not None and metadata is not None:
90	            # save_only_first_rank only works when use MLA, follow the same
91	            # semantics as LMCacheEngine and memory allocator.
92	            self.save_only_first_rank = (
93	                config.get_extra_config_value("save_only_first_rank", metadata.use_mla)
94	                and metadata.use_mla
95	            )
96	
97	    def _get_vllm_hash_func(self, hash_algorithm: str):
98	        """Get hash function from vLLM with version compatibility.
99	
100	        Tries multiple import paths to support different vLLM versions:
101	        - vllm.utils.hashing.get_hash_fn_by_name (>= PR#27151)
102	        - vllm.utils.get_hash_fn_by_name (< PR#27151)
103	        - Direct imports as fallback
104	        - sha256_cbor_64bit -> sha256_cbor rename (PR#23673)
105	        """
106	        # Try get_hash_fn_by_name from both locations (PR#27151)
107	        for module_path in ["vllm.utils.hashing", "vllm.utils"]:
108	            try:
109	                module = __import__(module_path, fromlist=["get_hash_fn_by_name"])
110	                get_hash_fn_by_name = module.get_hash_fn_by_name
111	                return self._try_get_hash(
112	                    get_hash_fn_by_name, hash_algorithm, module_path
113	                )
114	            except (ImportError, AttributeError, ValueError):
115	                continue
116	
117	        # Try direct imports as fallback (for older vLLM versions)
118	        func_names = (
119	            ["sha256_cbor", "sha256_cbor_64bit"]
120	            if hash_algorithm in ("sha256_cbor", "sha256_cbor_64bit")
121	            else [hash_algorithm]
122	        )
123	        for module_path in ["vllm.utils.hashing", "vllm.utils"]:
124	            for func_name in func_names:
125	                try:
126	                    module = __import__(module_path, fromlist=[func_name])
127	                    hash_func = getattr(module, func_name)
128	                    logger.info(
129	                        f"Loaded '{func_name}' from {module_path} (direct import)"
130	                    )
131	                    return hash_func
132	                except (ImportError, AttributeError):
133	                    continue
134	
135	        # Fallback to builtin hash
136	        logger.warning(
137	            f"Could not load '{hash_algorithm}' from vLLM. Using builtin hash. "
138	            "This may cause inconsistencies in distributed caching."
139	        )
140	
141	        # Check PYTHONHASHSEED when using builtin hash
142	        if os.getenv("PYTHONHASHSEED") is None:
143	            logger.warning(
144	                "Using builtin hash without PYTHONHASHSEED set. "
145	                "For production environments (non-testing scenarios), you MUST set "
146	                "PYTHONHASHSEED to ensure consistent hashing across processes. "
147	                "Example: export PYTHONHASHSEED=0"
148	            )
149	
150	        return hash
151	
152	    def _try_get_hash(self, get_hash_fn_by_name, hash_algorithm: str, module_name: str):
153	        """Try to get hash function, handling sha256_cbor_64bit rename."""
154	        # Handle sha256_cbor_64bit -> sha256_cbor rename (PR#23673)
155	        names_to_try = (
156	            ["sha256_cbor", "sha256_cbor_64bit"]
157	            if hash_algorithm in ("sha256_cbor", "sha256_cbor_64bit")
158	            else [hash_algorithm]
159	        )
160	
161	        for name in names_to_try:
162	            try:
163	                hash_func = get_hash_fn_by_name(name)
164	                logger.info(f"Loaded '{name}' from {module_name}")
165	                return hash_func
166	            except ValueError:
167	                continue
168	        raise ValueError(f"Hash function '{hash_algorithm}' not found in {module_name}")
169	
170	    @abc.abstractmethod
171	    def process_tokens(
172	        self,
173	        tokens: Optional[Union[torch.Tensor, List[int]]] = None,
174	        hashes: Optional[List[int]] = None,
175	        offsets: Optional[List[int]] = None,
176	        mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
177	        make_key: bool = True,
178	        request_configs: Optional[dict] = None,
179	    ) -> Iterable[ProcessTokensResult]:
180	        """Process the tokens and return the corresponding cache engine keys.
181	
182	        :param Optional[Union[torch.Tensor, List[int]]] tokens: The tokens to process.
183	
184	        :param Optional[List[int]] hashes: The hashes to process. If provided,
185	            it will be used instead of tokens to generate cache engine keys.
186	
187	        :param Optional[List[int]] offsets: The number of tokens in each chunk.
188	
189	        :param Optional[torch.Tensor] mask: The mask for the tokens. Should
190	            have the same length as tokens. And the mask should ALWAYS be like
191	            FFFFFTTTTTTT, where True means the tokens needs to be matched,
192	            and the Falses will ALWAYS be at the PREFIX of the tensor.
193	
194	        :param bool make_key: Whether to make the cache engine key or not.
195	            If False, the hash value will be returned instead.
196	
197	        :param Optional[dict] request_configs: The configs of the request.
198	
199	        :returns: A iterable of tuples with three elements. The first element
200	            is the start index of the tokens for the key. The second element
201	            is the end index of the tokens for the key. The third element is
202	            the cache engine key (or hash) for the tokens.
203	        """
204	
205	        raise NotImplementedError
206	
207	    def _make_key_by_hash(
208	        self, chunk_hash: int, request_configs: Optional[dict] = None
209	    ):
210	        assert self.metadata is not None
211	        # When save_only_first_rank is enabled (for MLA), we deliberately
212	        # collapse the CacheEngineKey.world_size to 1 so that cache keys
213	        # become world-size agnostic across compatible deployments.
214	        return CacheEngineKey(
215	            self.metadata.model_name,
216	            self.metadata.world_size if not self.save_only_first_rank else 1,
217	            self.metadata.worker_id,
218	            chunk_hash,
219	            self.metadata.kv_dtype,
220	            request_configs,
221	        )
222	
223	    def _canonicalize_hash_inputs(
224	        self,
225	        prefix_hash: Optional[int],
226	        tokens_tuple: Tuple[int, ...],
227	        extra_keys: Optional[List[Any]],
228	    ) -> Tuple[int, Tuple[int, ...], Tuple[Any, ...]]:
229	        """
230	        Canonicalize hash inputs so that semantically identical requests
231	        produce structurally identical hash inputs across instances.
232	        - prefix_hash: int or NONE_HASH if None
233	        - tokens_tuple: tuple of token IDs
234	        - extra_keys: tuple of additional keys, empty if None
235	        """
236	        return (
237	            prefix_hash if prefix_hash is not None else NONE_HASH,
238	            tokens_tuple,
239	            tuple(extra_keys) if extra_keys is not None else (),
240	        )
241	
242	    def _hash_tokens(
243	        self,
244	        tokens: Union[torch.Tensor, List[int]],
245	        prefix_hash: Optional[int] = None,
246	        extra_keys: Optional[list[Any]] = None,
247	    ) -> int:
248	        if isinstance(tokens, torch.Tensor):
249	            tokens_tuple = tuple(tokens.cpu().tolist())
250	        elif isinstance(tokens, list):
251	            tokens_tuple = tuple(tokens)
252	        else:
253	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported tokens type: {type(tokens)}")
254	
255	        # Ignore extra keys for now
256	        # Extra keys are for multi-modal inputs and
257	        # request specific metadata (e.g., LoRA ID).
258	        # Use default values for None to maintain a fixed tuple structure for hashing.
259	
260	        # Use helper to canonicalize inputs to ensure consistent hashing
261	        # This replaces the logic that was causing inconsistency
262	        canon_prefix, canon_tokens, canon_extra = self._canonicalize_hash_inputs(
263	            prefix_hash, tokens_tuple, extra_keys
264	        )
265	
266	        return self.hash_func((canon_prefix, canon_tokens, canon_extra))
267	
268	
269	class ChunkedTokenDatabase(TokenDatabase):
270	    def __init__(
271	        self,
272	        config: Optional[LMCacheEngineConfig] = None,
273	        metadata: Optional[LMCacheMetadata] = None,
274	    ):
275	        super(ChunkedTokenDatabase, self).__init__(config, metadata)
276	
277	        if config is not None:
278	            self.config = config
279	            self.chunk_size = config.chunk_size
280	
281	            # Check for cross-process cache sharing setup
282	            if os.getenv("PYTHONHASHSEED") is None:
283	                if config.remote_url is not None:
284	                    logger.warning(
285	                        "Centralized cache sharing detected "
286	                        "but PYTHONHASHSEED not set. "
287	                        "For consistent caching, set: export PYTHONHASHSEED=0 "
288	                        "before the engine starts."
289	                    )
290	                if config.enable_pd:
291	                    logger.error(
292	                        "P/D Disaggregation detected "
293	                        "but PYTHONHASHSEED not set. "
294	                        "For consistent caching, set: export PYTHONHASHSEED=0 "
295	                        "before the engine starts. "
296	                        "This will cause incorrect KV cache transfer."
297	                    )
298	        else:  # Default values
299	            self.config = None
300	            self.chunk_size = 256

45	@asynccontextmanager
46	async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
47	    """
48	    Manage the lifecycle of the LMCache HTTP server.
49	
50	    On startup: Initialize ZMQ server and cache engine.
51	    On shutdown: Clean up ZMQ server resources.
52	    """
53	    # Startup
54	    logger.info(
55	        "Starting LMCache HTTP server... (CUDA available: %s)",
56	        torch.cuda.is_available(),
57	    )
58	    mp_config = _configs["mp"]
59	    if mp_config.engine_type == "blend":
60	        # First Party
61	        from lmcache.v1.multiprocess.blend_server_v2 import run_cache_server
62	    else:
63	        # First Party
64	        from lmcache.v1.multiprocess.server import run_cache_server
65	
66	    zmq_server, engine = run_cache_server(
67	        mp_config=mp_config,
68	        storage_manager_config=_configs["storage_manager"],
69	        obs_config=_configs["observability"],
70	        return_engine=True,
71	    )
72	    app.state.zmq_server = zmq_server
73	    app.state.engine = engine
74	    logger.info("LMCache HTTP server initialized")
75	
76	    yield
77	
78	    # Shutdown
79	    logger.info("Shutting down LMCache HTTP server...")
80	    get_event_bus().stop()
81	    if hasattr(app.state, "zmq_server") and app.state.zmq_server is not None:
82	        app.state.zmq_server.close()
83	    logger.info("LMCache HTTP server stopped")

95	async def healthcheck(request: Request):
96	    """
97	    Health check endpoint for k8s liveness/readiness probes.
98	
99	    Checks:
100	        - HTTP server is alive (implicit: if you get a response)
101	        - Cache engine is alive
102	    """
103	    engine = getattr(request.app.state, "engine", None)
104	    if engine is None:
105	        return JSONResponse(
106	            status_code=503,
107	            content={"status": "unhealthy", "reason": "engine not initialized"},
108	        )
109

114	async def clear_cache(request: Request):
115	    """
116	    Force-clear all KV cache data stored in L1 (CPU) memory.
117	
118	    This clears all objects including those with active read/write locks.
119	    In-flight store or prefetch operations may be corrupted.
120	    """
121	    engine = getattr(request.app.state, "engine", None)
122	    if engine is None:
123	        return JSONResponse(
124	            status_code=503,
125	            content={"status": "error", "reason": "engine not initialized"},
126	        )
127	
128	    engine.clear()
129	    logger.info("Cache cleared via HTTP API")
130	    return {"status": "ok"}

133	@app.get("/api/status")

137	    MP components (L1 cache, L2 adapters, controllers, sessions).
138	    """
139	    engine = getattr(request.app.state, "engine", None)
140	    if engine is None:

143	            content={"error": "engine not initialized"},
144	        )

148	def run_http_server(
149	    http_config: HTTPFrontendConfig,
150	    mp_config: MPServerConfig,
151	    storage_manager_config: StorageManagerConfig,
152	    obs_config: ObservabilityConfig,
153	) -> None:
154	    """
155	    Run the LMCache HTTP server with integrated MP (ZMQ) server.
156	
157	    Args:
158	        http_config: Configuration for the HTTP frontend
159	        mp_config: Configuration for the ZMQ multiprocess server
160	        storage_manager_config: Configuration for the storage manager
161	        obs_config: Configuration for the observability stack
162	    """
163	    _configs["mp"] = mp_config
164	    _configs["storage_manager"] = storage_manager_config
165	    _configs["observability"] = obs_config
166	
167	    config = uvicorn.Config(
168	        app=app,
169	        host=http_config.http_host,
170	        port=http_config.http_port,
171	        log_level="info",
172	        access_log=True,
173	    )
174	    server = uvicorn.Server(config)
175	
176	    logger.info(
177	        "Starting LMCache HTTP server on http://%s:%d",
178	        http_config.http_host,
179	        http_config.http_port,
180	    )
181	    server.run()

200	    obs_config = parse_args_to_observability_config(args)

Here's a comprehensive summary of how LMCache works under the hood:

---

## LMCache Architecture Summary

LMCache is a **KV cache offloading and sharing system** designed to plug into LLM inference engines (primarily vLLM, with SGLang support). It intercepts KV caches after GPU prefill and stores them in a tiered memory hierarchy so future requests can skip recomputation for repeated token prefixes.

---

### Core Abstraction Stack

**1. Token Indexing — `token_database.py`**

Before any cache operation, tokens are converted into `CacheEngineKey`s. `ChunkedTokenDatabase` splits the token sequence into fixed-size chunks (default 256 tokens) and hashes each prefix incrementally using a pluggable hash function (sha256_cbor, blake3, or Python's builtin). The hash chain ensures prefix-dependent cache keys: chunk N's hash depends on all chunks 0..N-1. `PYTHONHASHSEED` must be fixed for distributed sharing. Keys embed `(model_name, world_size, worker_id, chunk_hash, kv_dtype, request_configs)`.

**2. Memory Objects & Allocators — `memory_management.py`**

`MemoryObj` is the universal unit of storage. The primary implementation, `TensorMemoryObj`, wraps a flat pinned CPU tensor with:
- **Ref-counting** (`ref_count_up/down`) — frees back to allocator when count hits zero
- **Pin-counting** (`pin/unpin`) — prevents eviction while in-flight (tracked by a global `PinMonitor` with timeout)
- Multiple **MemoryFormats**: `KV_2LTD`, `KV_T2D`, `KV_2TD`, `KV_MLA_FMT`, `BINARY`, etc.

The allocator hierarchy:
- **`AddressManager`**: A virtual address space manager with a sorted free-list and 4 KB-aligned allocations. Supports coalescing adjacent free blocks.
- **`TensorMemoryAllocator`**: Allocates pinned CPU tensors from a pre-reserved pool via `AddressManager`. Supports NUMA-aware placement and shared-memory (`shm`) backing.
- **`PagedTensorMemoryAllocator`**: Paged variant; allocates fixed-size pages.
- **`MixedMemoryAllocator`**: Combines the above with LRU eviction on the pool.
- **`CuFileMemoryAllocator`**: GDS (GPU-direct storage) variant backed by cuFile.
- **`GPUMemoryAllocator`**: For GPU-resident intermediate buffers.

**3. GPU Connectors — `gpu_connectors.py`**

`GPUConnectorInterface` is the bridge between vLLM's paged KV cache (on GPU) and LMCache's `MemoryObj` (on CPU/disk). The primary implementation, `VLLMPagedMemGPUConnectorV2`, uses custom CUDA kernels (`lmcache.c_ops`) to:
- **`batched_from_gpu`**: Gather KV blocks from vLLM's paged GPU tensors (indexed by page table/slot_mapping) → copy into `MemoryObj` via CUDA streams
- **`batched_to_gpu`**: Scatter stored KV tensors back into vLLM's paged buffer for a new request
- Handles MHA, MLA, flash-attention, flash-infer, and XPU layout variants. Layerwise connectors use a generator/send protocol to pipeline copy + store across layers.

**4. Cache Engine — `cache_engine.py` (2034 lines)**

`LMCacheEngine` is the central orchestrator. Its four main entry points:

| Method | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `store(tokens, mask, **kwargs)` | Tokenize → chunk → allocate `MemoryObj` → `gpu_connector.batched_from_gpu` → async `storage_manager.batched_put` |
| `store_layer(tokens, ...)` | Same but pipelines per-layer: copy layer N while putting layer N-1 |
| `retrieve(tokens, mask, **kwargs)` | Lookup chunks → `storage_manager.batched_get` → `gpu_connector.batched_to_gpu` → return boolean mask of hits |
| `retrieve_layer(tokens, ...)` | Layerwise: prefetch next layer while copying current to GPU |
| `lookup(tokens, pin=True)` | Pure cache existence check; optionally pins chunks to prevent eviction |

Cross-cutting concerns: health monitoring (`is_healthy()` / `mark_init_failed()`), freeze mode (read-only hot cache), KV events (audit log), NVTX profiling annotations, and stats monitoring via Prometheus.

**5. Storage Manager — `storage_backend/storage_manager.py` (1352 lines)**

`StorageManager` is the multi-tier router. It owns an `asyncio` event loop in a dedicated thread for async operations. Key behaviors:

- **`batched_put`**: Allocates memory in the primary `AllocatorBackend` (usually `LocalCPUBackend`), then copies to any secondary backends (remote Redis/disk/etc.) in parallel
- **`batched_get`**: Tries backends in priority order; on remote hit, auto-writes back to local CPU (write-through cache)
- **`async_lookup_and_prefetch`**: Used by the scheduler. Does a tier-by-tier async `batched_async_contains` then schedules `batched_get_non_blocking` coroutines. Enforces **prefix continuity** — if tier N is missing a chunk, all subsequent chunks from deeper tiers are dropped
- **`WeightedSemaphore`**: Prevents deadlock when multiple concurrent async loads compete for memory allocation budget
- Backend bypass: unhealthy backends are skipped via `_bypassed_backends` set

**6. Local CPU Backend — `storage_backend/local_cpu_backend.py` (810 lines)**

The hot in-process CPU DRAM cache. Backed by a pluggable cache policy (`LRU/LFU/FIFO/MRU`) over a dict (`hot_cache`). On `insert`, increments ref-count. On `remove` (eviction), decrements ref-count and sends an `EVICT` message to the cache controller cluster (via `BatchedMessageSender`). Pin/unpin is forwarded to the underlying `MemoryObj`. Also detects available NUMA memory and reserves an appropriate fraction.

**7. Multiprocess Mode — `multiprocess/server.py` + `http_server.py`**

For multi-process serving (one LMCache server shared across multiple vLLM workers):

- **`MPCacheEngine`** runs in a dedicated process, receiving requests over **ZeroMQ** from vLLM worker processes
- Workers send `IPCCacheEngineKey` messages (store/retrieve/lookup/prefetch) and pass IPC event handles for CUDA synchronization
- The server maps token sequences to hashes via `TokenHasher` (blake3 by default), then calls the distributed `StorageManager` (L1/L2 architecture)
- **`http_server.py`** (the file you had open) wraps this in a **FastAPI/uvicorn** HTTP server, exposing `/api/healthcheck`, `/api/status`, and `/api/clear-cache`. On startup it launches either the standard `MPCacheEngine` or a `BlendCacheEngine` depending on config

**8. Blend Mode — `blend_server_v2.py`**

Goes beyond prefix matching: uses a rolling polynomial hash (`BlendTokenRangeMatcher`) to detect matching token windows anywhere in a sequence, then prefetches and splices stored KV chunks at arbitrary offsets — enabling KV reuse even when the shared context is not a strict prefix.

---

### Data Flow (Store Path)

```
vLLM GPU KV cache (paged blocks)
       │
       ▼ gpu_connector.batched_from_gpu (CUDA stream, custom kernel)
MemoryObj (pinned CPU tensor, ref_count=1)
       │
       ▼ storage_manager.batched_put
  ┌────┴─────────────────────────────┐
  │ LocalCPUBackend (hot cache dict) │
  └────┬─────────────────────────────┘
       │ async copy if remote backend configured
       ▼
  RemoteBackend (Redis/Valkey/S3/Infinistore...)
```

### Data Flow (Retrieve Path)

```
tokens → ChunkedTokenDatabase → CacheEngineKeys
       │
       ▼ storage_manager.batched_get (LocalCPU first, then remote)
MemoryObj (pinned CPU, ref_count incremented)
       │
       ▼ gpu_connector.batched_to_gpu (CUDA stream, custom kernel)
vLLM GPU paged KV cache (filled in)
       │
       ▼ returns bool mask of which tokens were hit
```

### Key Design Decisions

- **Chunked prefix hashing**: cache granularity is a fixed chunk of tokens; a hit requires a full unbroken prefix up to a chunk boundary
- **Ref-counting + pin-counting for memory safety**: no GC pauses; safe eviction even with concurrent readers
- **Async put, sync get**: stores are fire-and-forget; retrieves block until data is available (with optional async prefetch via `WeightedSemaphore`)
- **Layerwise store/retrieve**: overlaps GPU→CPU transfer of layer N with CPU→storage of layer N-1, hiding I/O latency
- **Health monitoring & freeze mode**: graceful degradation when backends fail; freeze protects the hot cache during critical windows
- **NUMA-awareness**: pinned memory allocations target the NUMA node closest to the GPU
You mentioned the token hashing granularity. What exact chunk size did you say LMCache uses, and why does that matter for reuse?

The default chunk size is **256 tokens**, set in `ChunkedTokenDatabase.__init__` at `token_database.py:279`:

```python
self.chunk_size = config.chunk_size  # defaults to 256
```

**Why it matters for reuse:**

Cache hits are all-or-nothing at chunk boundaries. A request must share a complete unbroken prefix of chunks with a cached sequence to get any reuse. Concretely:

- If two requests share the first 300 tokens, only **1 chunk (256 tokens)** is reused — the remaining 44 tokens don't form a complete chunk and are recomputed
- If a cached sequence has 512 tokens but the new request diverges at token 257, you get **1 chunk** back, not 2
- The `StorageManager` enforces prefix continuity: if chunk N is missing, chunks N+1, N+2, ... are dropped even if they're present in cache

**The tradeoff:**

| Smaller chunks | Larger chunks |
|---|---|
| Higher reuse rate (finer granularity) | Fewer cache entries to manage |
| More keys to hash/lookup | Lower overhead per lookup |
| More memory fragmentation | Wastes more compute on partial matches |

256 is a common middle ground for transformer workloads where typical shared system prompts or few-shot examples are hundreds to low thousands of tokens. The value is configurable via `LMCacheEngineConfig.chunk_size`.

I now have a thorough picture of the repository. Here is a complete summary:

---

## LMCache Repository Structure

### Top-Level Layout

```
/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/
├── lmcache/          # Main Python package (all source code)
├── tests/            # Test suite mirroring lmcache/ structure
├── benchmarks/       # Benchmark scripts (QA, RAG, storage I/O)
├── examples/         # Usage examples (disagg prefill, blend KV, etc.)
├── csrc/             # C/CUDA extension source code
├── rust/             # Rust components
├── docker/           # Docker deployment files
├── docs/             # Sphinx documentation
├── tools/            # Developer utilities
├── requirements/     # Pinned dependency files
├── pyproject.toml    # Build/package config
└── setup.py
```

### Main Package Sub-directories (`lmcache/`)

```
lmcache/
├── v1/                    # Active v1 engine (primary codebase)
│   ├── storage_backend/   # Multi-tier storage backends + connectors
│   ├── distributed/       # Distributed L1/L2 cache management (MP mode)
│   ├── multiprocess/      # ZMQ-based multi-process server + blend server
│   ├── gpu_connector/     # GPU↔CPU KV cache I/O connectors
│   ├── cache_controller/  # Central cluster KV-cache controller
│   ├── internal_api_server/ # Per-worker FastAPI/HTTP management API
│   ├── lookup_client/     # Cache lookup client (async, bloom filter, etc.)
│   ├── compute/           # Attention kernels, blend (non-prefix reuse), models
│   ├── transfer_channel/  # NIXL/socket-based P2P transfer channels
│   ├── offload_server/    # ZMQ offload server for async KV writes
│   ├── health_monitor/    # Backend health checking and fallback
│   ├── mp_observability/  # OpenTelemetry event bus for multiprocess mode
│   └── ...
├── integration/           # vLLM and SGLang adapters
├── storage_backend/       # Legacy serde and evictor utilities
├── server/                # Legacy standalone server
├── cli/                   # Command-line interface (ping, describe, bench, etc.)
└── tools/                 # Controller benchmark, MP status viewer
```

---

## 9 Most Important Core Python Files

### 1. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/cache_engine.py` (2034 lines)
The central caching engine class `LMCacheEngine`. Implements the full retrieve/store pipeline: token processing, chunk-based prefix hashing, KV eviction, GPU↔storage data flow, async put/get dispatch, and NVTX profiling hooks. This is the heart of LMCache.

### 2. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/memory_management.py` (2621 lines)
Defines all memory abstractions: `MemoryFormat` enums (KV_2LTD, KV_T2D, BINARY, etc.), `MemoryObj`/`TensorMemoryObj`, and a hierarchy of allocators — `TensorMemoryAllocator`, `PagedTensorMemoryAllocator`, `MixedMemoryAllocator`, `CuFileMemoryAllocator`, `GPUMemoryAllocator`. Thread-safety via a `synchronized` decorator. This is the memory subsystem.

### 3. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/gpu_connector/gpu_connectors.py` (1904 lines)
Defines `GPUConnectorInterface` (abstract) and concrete GPU connectors that move KV cache tensors between vLLM's GPU page cache and LMCache's memory objects (`to_gpu` / `from_gpu`). Handles various layout transformations (MHA, MLA, flash-attn, flash-infer, XPU).

### 4. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/storage_backend/storage_manager.py` (1352 lines)
`StorageManager` orchestrates the multi-tier local storage hierarchy (hot CPU cache + remote backend). Implements async get/put with futures, event-driven eviction notification, integration with the cache controller worker, and Prometheus metrics. The glue layer between the cache engine and individual backends.

### 5. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/storage_backend/local_cpu_backend.py` (810 lines)
`LocalCPUBackend` — the in-process CPU DRAM hot cache. Uses a pluggable cache policy (LRU/LFU/FIFO/MRU), a `MixedMemoryAllocator` for tensor storage, and implements blocking/async get and insert with thread safety. The L1 cache for the single-process engine.

### 6. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/storage_backend/remote_backend.py` (600 lines)
`RemoteBackend` — handles serialization and async transfer of KV chunks to/from a remote store (Redis, Valkey, S3, Infinistore, Mooncake, etc.) via a pluggable `RemoteConnector`. Manages reconnection on failure and integrates with the local CPU backend as a write-through / read-fill layer.

### 7. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/multiprocess/server.py` (1002 lines)
The multi-process ZMQ server (`MPServer`) that runs in a dedicated process. Receives KV cache put/get/prefetch requests from vLLM worker processes via a message queue, dispatches them to the `StorageManager` (distributed mode), and manages GPU cache context, IPC memory, and threading.

### 8. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/multiprocess/blend_server_v2.py` (882 lines)
The "blend" server enabling KV reuse at *arbitrary token sub-sequences* (not only prefixes). Uses a rolling polynomial hash (`BlendTokenRangeMatcher`) to detect matching windows across requests, then prefetches and splices stored KV chunks into new requests' cache buffers at the right offsets.

### 9. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/distributed/storage_manager.py` (514 lines)
`StorageManager` for the distributed/multiprocess mode. Coordinates a two-tier architecture: L1 (fast, node-local GPU/CPU memory via `L1Manager`) and L2 (remote/offloaded storage via `L2AdapterInterface`). Manages `PrefetchHandle` tracking, eviction, and store policies across tiers.

### 10. `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/manager.py` (539 lines)
`LMCacheManager` — the unified lifecycle manager that composes all subsystems (engine, lookup client/server, offload server, internal API server, health monitor, plugin launcher) for a given worker. Integrates with a `BaseServiceFactory` that determines which components each worker type (prefill, decode, standalone) needs.

---

### Additional Notable Files

- `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/token_database.py` (551 lines) — `ChunkedTokenDatabase` and `SegmentTokenDatabase`: convert input token sequences into `CacheEngineKey` lists via rolling prefix hashes; the indexing layer.
- `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/config.py` (810 lines) — `LMCacheEngineConfig`: YAML/env-var/CLI-override configuration system with alias resolution and deprecation warnings.
- `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/distributed/l1_manager.py` (807 lines) — `L1Manager`: object-level state machine (write locks, read TTL locks, temporary objects) for the distributed L1 cache, backed by `L1MemoryManager`.
- `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/lazy_memory_allocator.py` (270 lines) — `LazyMemoryAllocator`: defers physical allocation until first write; used with NUMA-aware placement.
- `/Users/jiayuechen/Desktop/LMCache/lmcache/v1/kv_layer_groups.py` (230 lines) — `KVLayerGroupInfo` dataclass grouping transformer layers that share KV shape/dtype (important for MLA vs. MHA heterogeneous models).

219	        if not config.py_enable_gc:
220	            gc.disable()
221	
222	        # Health monitor reference (injected by LMCacheManager)
223	        self._health_monitor: Optional["HealthMonitor"] = None
224	
225	        # Flag to indicate if initialization failed (irrecoverable error)
226	        self._init_failed = False
227	
228	    def set_health_monitor(self, health_monitor: "HealthMonitor") -> None:
229	        """
230	        Set the health monitor reference.
231	
232	        This is called by LMCacheManager after creating the HealthMonitor
233	        to inject the reference into the engine.
234	
235	        Args:
236	            health_monitor: The HealthMonitor instance from LMCacheManager
237	        """
238	        self._health_monitor = health_monitor
239	
240	    def is_healthy(self) -> bool:
241	        """
242	        Check if the LMCache system is healthy.
243	
244	        This method returns False if:
245	        - Initialization failed (irrecoverable error)
246	        - HealthMonitor reports unhealthy
247	
248	        If no health monitor is set and initialization succeeded,
249	        it returns True (assume healthy).
250	
251	        Returns:
252	            bool: True if healthy, False otherwise
253	        """
254	        if self._init_failed:
255	            return False
256	        if self._health_monitor is not None:
257	            return self._health_monitor.is_healthy()
258	        return True
259	
260	    def _get_req_id(self, kwargs: dict) -> str:
261	        """Extracts request ID from kwargs for logging."""
262	        return kwargs.get("req_id", "unspecified")
263	
264	    def mark_init_failed(self, reason: str = "") -> None:
265	        """
266	        Mark the engine as having failed initialization.
267	
268	        This is called by LMCacheManager when an irrecoverable error occurs
269	        during initialization or post_init. Once marked, is_healthy() will
270	        always return False, causing the system to fall back to recomputation.
271	
272	        Args:
273	            reason: Optional reason string for logging
274	        """
275	        self._init_failed = True
276	        if reason:
277	            logger.error("LMCacheEngine marked as init failed: %s", reason)
278	        else:
279	            logger.error("LMCacheEngine marked as init failed")
280	
281	    def post_init(self, **kwargs) -> None:
282	        if not self.post_inited:
283	            logger.info("Post initializing LMCacheEngine")
284	            lookup_server_worker_ids = self.config.get_lookup_server_worker_ids(
285	                self.metadata.use_mla, self.metadata.world_size
286	            )
287	            if (
288	                self.lmcache_worker is not None
289	                or self.use_layerwise
290	                or not self.save_only_first_rank
291	                or self.metadata.is_first_rank()
292	                or len(lookup_server_worker_ids) == 0
293	                or self.metadata.worker_id in lookup_server_worker_ids
294	            ):
295	                logger.info(
296	                    f"Initialize storage manager on rank {self.metadata.worker_id}, "
297	                    f"use layerwise: {self.use_layerwise},"
298	                    f"save only first rank: {self.save_only_first_rank}"
299	                )
300	                async_lookup_server = kwargs.get("async_lookup_server", None)

37	# Helper functions for thread safety
38	def synchronized(lock_attr_name):
39	    """
40	    Decorator to make a method thread-safe by acquiring the lock
41	    specified by lock_attr_name on the instance.
42	    """
43	
44	    def decorator(method):
45	        @wraps(method)
46	        def wrapper(self, *args, **kwargs):
47	            lock = getattr(self, lock_attr_name)
48	            with lock:
49	                return method(self, *args, **kwargs)
50	
51	        return wrapper
52	
53	    return decorator

56	class MemoryFormat(Enum):
57	    UNDEFINED = 0
58	    """[2, num_layers, num_tokens, hidden_dim]
59	    """
60	    # KV_BLOB = 1
61	    KV_2LTD = auto()
62	    """[num_tokens, 2, hidden_dim]
63	    """
64	    # LAYER_KV_BLOB = 2
65	    KV_T2D = auto()
66	    """[2, num_tokens, hidden_dim]
67	    """
68	
69	    KV_2TD = auto()
70	    """Compressed binary array format
71	    """
72	    BINARY = auto()
73	
74	    BINARY_BUFFER = auto()
75	
76	    KV_MLA_FMT = auto()
77	    """[1, num_layers, num_tokens, aligned_head_size]
78	    """
79	
80	    def token_dim(self) -> int:
81	        if self == MemoryFormat.KV_2LTD:
82	            return 2
83	        elif self == MemoryFormat.KV_T2D:
84	            return 1
85	        elif self == MemoryFormat.KV_2TD:
86	            return 0
87	        elif self == MemoryFormat.BINARY:
88	            return 0
89	        elif self == MemoryFormat.BINARY_BUFFER:
90	            return 0
91	        elif self == MemoryFormat.KV_MLA_FMT:
92	            return 2
93	        return 0

107	@dataclass
108	class MemoryObjMetadata:
109	    # TODO(chunxiaozheng): use shapes and dtypes to replace shape and dtype
110	    # The 'logical' shape of the tensor
111	    shape: torch.Size
112	
113	    # The 'logical' dtype of the tensor
114	    dtype: Optional[torch.dtype]
115	
116	    # The 'physical address' of the tensor
117	    address: int
118	
119	    # The 'physical size' in bytes of the allocated memory
120	    phy_size: int
121	
122	    # Reference count
123	    ref_count: int
124	
125	    # Whether the object is pinned and cannot be evicted
126	    # lookup pins are temporary
127	    # cache controller pins are persistent
128	    pin_count: int = 0
129	
130	    # The 'logical' format of the tensor
131	    fmt: MemoryFormat = MemoryFormat.UNDEFINED
132	
133	    # Positions when the cache is stored
134	    cached_positions: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
135	
136	    # shapes and dtypes should be used in the future
137	    shapes: Optional[list[torch.Size]] = None
138	    dtypes: Optional[list[torch.dtype]] = None
139	
140	    def to_dict(self):
141	        # Note(Kuntai): this is used for serializing MemoryObjMetadata via
142	        # msgpack.
143	        return {
144	            "__type__": "MemoryObjMetadata",
145	            "shape": list(self.shape),  # torch.Size -> list
146	            "dtype": str(self.dtype) if self.dtype else None,
147	            "address": self.address,
148	            "phy_size": self.phy_size,
149	            "ref_count": self.ref_count,
150	            "fmt": self.fmt.value,
151	            "shapes": [list(shape) for shape in self.shapes] if self.shapes else None,
152	            "dtypes": [str(dtype) for dtype in self.dtypes] if self.dtypes else None,
153	        }
154	
155	    @staticmethod
156	    def from_dict(d):
157	        dtype_str = d["dtype"]
158	        dtype = getattr(torch, dtype_str.replace("torch.", "")) if dtype_str else None
159	        shapes_list = d["shapes"]
160	        shapes = [torch.Size(s) for s in shapes_list] if shapes_list else None
161	        dtypes_list = d["dtypes"]
162	        dtypes = (
163	            [getattr(torch, d_str.replace("torch.", "")) for d_str in dtypes_list]
164	            if dtypes_list
165	            else None
166	        )
167	        return MemoryObjMetadata(
168	            shape=torch.Size(d["shape"]),
169	            dtype=dtype,
170	            address=d["address"],
171	            phy_size=d["phy_size"],
172	            ref_count=d["ref_count"],
173	            fmt=MemoryFormat(d["fmt"]),
174	            shapes=shapes,
175	            dtypes=dtypes,
176	        )
177	
178	    def get_size(self) -> int:
179	        if self.shapes is not None and self.dtypes is not None:
180	            return get_size_bytes(self.shapes, self.dtypes)
181	        return self.shape.numel() * self.dtype.itemsize  # type: ignore

310	    def freeze(self, enabled: bool) -> None:
311	        """
312	        Set the freeze mode for the cache engine.
313	
314	        When freeze mode is enabled:
315	        - All store operations will be skipped (no new data stored)
316	        - Only local_cpu backend will be used for retrieval
317	        - No admit/evict messages will be generated
318	        This protects the local_cpu hot cache from changes.
319	
320	        Args:
321	            enabled (bool): Whether to enable freeze mode
322	        """
323	        if self.storage_manager is not None:
324	            self.storage_manager.set_freeze(enabled)

327	        """
328	        Get the current freeze mode status.

330	        Returns:
331	            bool: True if freeze mode is enabled, False otherwise
332	        """
333	        if self.storage_manager is not None:
334	            return self.storage_manager.is_frozen()
335	        return False

361	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
362	    @torch.inference_mode()
363	    def store(
364	        self,
365	        tokens: Optional[Union[torch.Tensor, list[int]]] = None,
366	        hashes: Optional[List[int]] = None,
367	        offsets: Optional[List[int]] = None,
368	        mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
369	        **kwargs,
370	    ) -> None:
371	        """Store the tokens/hashes and mask into the cache engine.
372	
373	        :param Optional[torch.Tensor] tokens: The tokens of the corresponding KV caches.
374	
375	        :param Optional[List[int]] hashes: The hashes of the corresponding KV caches.
376	
377	        :param Optional[torch.Tensor] mask: The mask for the tokens. Should
378	            have the same length as tokens. And the mask should ALWAYS be like
379	            FFFFFTTTTTTT, where True means the tokens needs to be matched,
380	            and the Falses will ALWAYS be at the PREFIX of the tensor.
381	
382	        :param **kwargs: The additional arguments for the storage backend which
383	            will be passed into the gpu_connector.
384	            Should include KV cache specific information (e.g., paged KV buffer
385	            and the page tables).
386	
387	        :raises: ValueError if the number of Falses in the mask is not a
388	            multiple of the chunk size.
389	        """
390	        # Health check: block operation if LMCache is unhealthy
391	        if not self.is_healthy():
392	            logger.warning("LMCache is unhealthy, skipping store operation")
393	            return
394	
395	        assert self.gpu_connector is not None, (
396	            "gpu_connector is required for store operation"
397	        )
398	
399	        if self._is_passive():
400	            logger.debug(f"rank={self.metadata.worker_id} ignore store")
401	            return
402	
403	        assert self.storage_manager is not None
404	
405	        # Get req_id for logging
406	        req_id = self._get_req_id(kwargs)
407	
408	        # Initialize num_to_store_tokens to avoid reference before assignment
409	        num_to_store_tokens = 0
410	
411	        if mask is not None:
412	            num_to_store_tokens = torch.sum(mask).item()
413	        elif tokens is not None:
414	            num_to_store_tokens = len(tokens)
415	        elif hashes is not None:
416	            assert offsets is not None, (
417	                "Offsets should be set when hashes are provided during store"
418	            )
419	            num_to_store_tokens = sum(offsets)
420	            kwargs["slot_mapping"] = torch.tensor(
421	                kwargs["slot_mapping"], dtype=torch.long, device="cuda"
422	            )
423	
424	        assert tokens is not None or hashes is not None, (
425	            "Either 'tokens' or 'hashes' must be provided."
426	        )
427	
428	        # KVCache Check logging
429	        self._log_kvcache_for_check(
430	            operation="Store",
431	            kwargs=kwargs,
432	            token_count=num_to_store_tokens,
433	            require_req_id=False,
434	        )
435	
436	        # Check if freeze mode is enabled
437	        if self.is_frozen():
438	            logger.debug(
439	                "Freeze mode enabled, skipping store operation for %d tokens",
440	                num_to_store_tokens,
441	            )
442	            return
443	
444	        store_stats = self.stats_monitor.on_store_request(num_to_store_tokens)
445	
446	        starts: List[int] = []
447	        ends: List[int] = []
448	        keys: List[CacheEngineKey] = []
449	        memory_objs: List[MemoryObj] = []
450	
451	        tot_kv_size = 0
452	        tot_token_num = 0
453	
454	        request_configs = kwargs.get("request_configs")
455	        if request_configs is not None and len(request_configs) != 0:
456	            assert isinstance(request_configs, dict)
457	
458	        with store_stats.profile_process_tokens():
459	            prev_key = 0
460	            for start, end, key in self.token_database.process_tokens(
461	                tokens,
462	                hashes,
463	                offsets,
464	                mask,
465	                request_configs=request_configs,
466	            ):
467	                assert isinstance(key, CacheEngineKey)
468	                # Allocate the memory object
469	                num_tokens = end - start
470	                kv_shapes = self.metadata.get_shapes(num_tokens)
471	                kv_dtypes = self.metadata.get_dtypes()
472	
473	                # TODO (Jiayi): should be batched in the future
474	                memory_obj = self.storage_manager.allocate(
475	                    kv_shapes,
476	                    kv_dtypes,
477	                    busy_loop=self.config.get_extra_config_value(
478	                        "force_store_wait", False
479	                    ),
480	                    fmt=self.fmt,
481	                )
482	                if memory_obj is None:
483	                    logger.warning(
484	                        "Local cpu memory under pressure so"
485	                        " choosing to store only "
486	                        f" {len(memory_objs)}"
487	                        " total chunks of KV cache."
488	                    )
489	                    break
490	
491	                starts.append(start)
492	                ends.append(end)
493	                keys.append(key)
494	                memory_objs.append(memory_obj)
495	                tot_kv_size += memory_obj.get_size()
496	                tot_token_num += num_tokens
497	
498	                # Create KV event
499	                if self.kv_events_enabled:
500	                    stored_event = CacheStoreEvent(
501	                        block_hashes=[key.chunk_hash],
502	                        parent_block_hash=None if start == 0 else prev_key,
503	                        token_ids=[],
504	                        block_size=num_tokens,
505	                        lora_id=None,
506	                        medium="cpu",
507	                        lora_name=None,
508	                    )
509	                    if tokens is not None:
510	                        stored_event.token_ids = convert_tokens_to_list(
511	                            tokens,
512	                            start,
513	                            end,
514	                        )
515	                        if isinstance(tokens, torch.Tensor):
516	                            stored_event.medium = tokens.device
517	                    elif hashes is not None:
518	                        stored_event.token_ids = hashes[start : end + 1]
519	                    logger.debug(
520	                        (
521	                            "Added kv cache event '%s' to kv cache events queue"
522	                            % stored_event
523	                        )
524	                    )
525	                    self.kv_events.append(stored_event)
526	                    prev_key = key.chunk_hash
527	
528	        # memory_objs might be empty, directly return to avoid sending tokens
529	        if not memory_objs:
530	            return
531	
532	        with store_stats.profile_from_gpu():
533	            self.gpu_connector.batched_from_gpu(memory_objs, starts, ends, **kwargs)
534	
535	        with store_stats.profile_put():
536	            transfer_spec = kwargs.get("transfer_spec", None)
537	            # TODO: we implicitly rely on batched_put to call ref_count_down
538	            # this management should be done in a cleaner way
539	            self.storage_manager.batched_put(
540	                keys,
541	                memory_objs,
542	                transfer_spec=transfer_spec,
543	                location=self.store_location,
544	            )
545	
546	        self.stats_monitor.on_store_finished(
547	            store_stats,
548	            tot_token_num,
549	        )
550	        tot_time = store_stats.time_to_store()
551	
552	        logger.info(
553	            "[req_id=%s] Stored %d out of total %d tokens. "
554	            "size: %.4f GB, cost %.4f ms, throughput: %.4f GB/s; "
555	            "offload_time: %.4f ms, put_time: %.4f ms",
556	            req_id,
557	            tot_token_num,
558	            num_to_store_tokens,
559	            tot_kv_size / 1024**3,
560	            tot_time * 1000,
561	            tot_kv_size / tot_time / 1024**3 if tot_time > 0 else 0,
562	            (store_stats.process_tokens_time + store_stats.from_gpu_time) * 1000,
563	            store_stats.put_time * 1000,
564	        )

566	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
567	    @torch.inference_mode()
568	    def store_layer(
569	        self,
570	        tokens: Union[torch.Tensor, list[int]],
571	        mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
572	        **kwargs,
573	    ) -> Generator[None, None, None]:
574	        """
575	        Store the KV cache in a layerwise manner.
576	
577	        :param torch.Tensor tokens: The tokens of the corresponding KV caches.
578	
579	        :param Optional[torch.Tensor] mask: The mask for the tokens. Should
580	            have the same length as tokens. And the mask should ALWAYS be like
581	            FFFFFTTTTTTT, where True means the tokens needs to be matched.
582	
583	        :param **kwargs: The additional arguments for the storage backend which
584	            will be passed into the gpu_connector.
585	
586	        return: A generator that yields None. In the first iteration, the
587	            generator allocates the memory objects for all layers and moves
588	            the KV cache of the first layer from GPU to CPU. In the next
589	            iterations, it moves the KV cache of layer i from GPU to the memory
590	            objects (on CPU) and puts the memory objects of layer i-1 to the
591	            storage backends. In the last iteration, it puts the memory objects
592	            of the last layer to the storage backends.
593	        """
594	        # Health check: block operation if LMCache is unhealthy
595	        if not self.is_healthy():
596	            logger.warning("LMCache is unhealthy, skipping store_layer operation")
597	            return
598	
599	        assert self.storage_manager is not None
600	        assert self.gpu_connector is not None, (
601	            "gpu_connector is required for store_layer operation"
602	        )
603	
604	        # Get req_id for logging
605	        req_id = self._get_req_id(kwargs)
606	
607	        if mask is not None:
608	            num_to_store_tokens = torch.sum(mask).item()
609	        else:
610	            num_to_store_tokens = len(tokens)
611	
612	        # KVCache Check logging
613	        self._log_kvcache_for_check(
614	            operation="Layerwise store",
615	            kwargs=kwargs,
616	            token_count=num_to_store_tokens,
617	            require_req_id=True,
618	        )
619	
620	        monitor_req_id = self.stats_monitor.on_store_request(num_to_store_tokens)
621	
622	        # Check if freeze mode is enabled
623	        if self.is_frozen():
624	            logger.debug(
625	                "Freeze mode enabled, skipping store_layer for %d tokens",
626	                num_to_store_tokens,
627	            )
628	            # Still need to yield to avoid StopIteration
629	            for layer_id in range(self.num_layers):
630	                yield
631	            return
632	
633	        starts = []
634	        ends = []
635	        keys = []
636	        memory_objs = []
637	        tot_token_num = 0
638	        kv_dtype = self.metadata.kv_dtype
639	        request_configs = kwargs.get("request_configs")
640	        if request_configs is not None and len(request_configs) != 0:
641	            assert isinstance(request_configs, dict)
642	
643	        prev_key = 0
644	        for start, end, key in self.token_database.process_tokens(
645	            tokens=tokens, mask=mask, request_configs=request_configs
646	        ):
647	            assert isinstance(key, CacheEngineKey)
648	
649	            keys_multi_layer = key.split_layers(self.num_layers)
650	            # Only check the first layer
651	            if self.storage_manager.contains(
652	                keys_multi_layer[0], self.retrieve_locations
653	            ):
654	                continue
655	
656	            # Allocate the memory object
657	            num_tokens = end - start
658	            kv_shape_single_layer = self.gpu_connector.get_shape(num_tokens)
659	
660	            memory_objs_multi_layer = self.storage_manager.batched_allocate(
661	                kv_shape_single_layer,
662	                kv_dtype,
663	                batch_size=self.num_layers,
664	                fmt=self.fmt,
665	                busy_loop=self.config.get_extra_config_value("force_store_wait", False),
666	            )
667	
668	            if memory_objs_multi_layer is None:
669	                logger.warning(
670	                    "Local cpu memory under pressure so"
671	                    " choosing to not store the KV cache."
672	                )
673	                break
674	
675	            starts.append(start)
676	            ends.append(end)
677	            keys.append(keys_multi_layer)
678	            memory_objs.append(memory_objs_multi_layer)
679	            tot_token_num += num_tokens
680	
681	            # Create KV event
682	            if self.kv_events_enabled and tokens is not None:
683	                stored_event = CacheStoreEvent(
684	                    block_hashes=[key.chunk_hash],
685	                    parent_block_hash=None if start == 0 else prev_key,
686	                    token_ids=[],
687	                    block_size=num_tokens,
688	                    lora_id=None,
689	                    medium="cpu",
690	                    lora_name=None,
691	                )
692	                if tokens is not None:
693	                    stored_event.token_ids = convert_tokens_to_list(
694	                        tokens,
695	                        start,
696	                        end,
697	                    )
698	                    if isinstance(tokens, torch.Tensor):
699	                        stored_event.medium = tokens.device

374	def _resolve_pinned_alloc_free(
375	    numa_mapping: Optional[NUMAMapping] = None,
376	    shm_name: Optional[str] = None,
377	    size: Optional[int] = None,
378	) -> Tuple[
379	    tuple,  # (alloc_fn, *alloc_args)
380	    tuple,  # (free_fn, *free_args_after_ptr)
381	]:
382	    """Resolve the alloc/free function pair based on memory type.
383	
384	    Returns:
385	        A tuple of (alloc_info, free_info) where:
386	        - alloc_info: (alloc_fn, *args) to call as alloc_fn(size, *args)
387	        - free_info: (free_fn, *args) to call as free_fn(ptr, *args)
388	    """
389	    if shm_name:
390	        return (
391	            (lmc_ops.alloc_shm_pinned_ptr, shm_name),
392	            (lmc_ops.free_shm_pinned_ptr, size, shm_name),
393	        )
394	    elif numa_mapping:
395	        if torch.cuda.is_available():
396	            current_device_id = torch.cuda.current_device()
397	        else:
398	            current_device_id = 0
399	        gpu_to_numa_mapping = numa_mapping.gpu_to_numa_mapping
400	        assert current_device_id in gpu_to_numa_mapping, (
401	            f"Current device {current_device_id} is not in the GPU NUMA mapping."
402	        )
403	        numa_id = gpu_to_numa_mapping[current_device_id]
404	        return (
405	            (lmc_ops.alloc_pinned_numa_ptr, numa_id),
406	            (lmc_ops.free_pinned_numa_ptr, size),
407	        )
408	    else:
409	        return (
410	            (lmc_ops.alloc_pinned_ptr, 0),
411	            (lmc_ops.free_pinned_ptr,),
412	        )
413	
414	
415	def _allocate_cpu_memory(
416	    size: int,
417	    numa_mapping: Optional[NUMAMapping] = None,
418	    shm_name: Optional[str] = None,
419	) -> torch.Tensor:
420	    if size == 0:
421	        return torch.empty(0, dtype=torch.uint8)
422	
423	    alloc_info, _ = _resolve_pinned_alloc_free(
424	        numa_mapping,
425	        shm_name,
426	    )
427	    alloc_fn, *alloc_args = alloc_info
428	    ptr = alloc_fn(size, *alloc_args)
429	
430	    array_type = ctypes.c_uint8 * size
431	    buf = array_type.from_address(ptr)
432	    buffer = torch.frombuffer(buf, dtype=torch.uint8)
433	
434	    return buffer
435	
436	
437	def _free_cpu_memory(
438	    buffer: torch.Tensor,
439	    size: int | None = None,
440	    numa_mapping: Optional[NUMAMapping] = None,
441	    shm_name: Optional[str] = None,
442	) -> None:
443	    if torch.cuda.is_available():
444	        torch.cuda.synchronize()
445	
446	    _, free_info = _resolve_pinned_alloc_free(
447	        numa_mapping,
448	        shm_name,
449	        size=size,
450	    )
451	    free_fn, *free_args = free_info
452	    free_fn(buffer.data_ptr(), *free_args)
453	
454	
455	def _allocate_gpu_memory(
456	    size: int,
457	    device: str,
458	) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
459	    page_size = os.sysconf("SC_PAGESIZE")
460	
461	    # Over-allocate
462	    base_buffer = torch.empty(size + page_size, dtype=torch.uint8, device=device)
463	    offset = -base_buffer.data_ptr() % page_size
464	
465	    # Make aligned view
466	    aligned_buffer = base_buffer[offset : offset + size]
467	
468	    # Need to return the base buffer as well in order to prevent GC
469	    return base_buffer, aligned_buffer

472	class TensorMemoryObj(MemoryObj):
473	    """
474	    Wraps a raw flat tensor with some metadata
475	    """
476	
477	    monitor = LMCStatsMonitor.GetOrCreate()
478	
479	    def __init__(
480	        self,
481	        raw_data: torch.Tensor,
482	        metadata: MemoryObjMetadata,
483	        parent_allocator: Optional["MemoryAllocatorInterface"],
484	    ):
485	        assert metadata.dtype is not None, "dtype must be specified for TensorMemoryObj"
486	        super().__init__(metadata)
487	        self.raw_data = raw_data
488	        self.valid = True
489	        self.lock = threading.Lock()
490	        self.parent_allocator = parent_allocator
491	        # Calculate the prefix sum of the group sizes
492	        # If there are two groups, the prefix sum will be
493	        # [0, size_of_group_1, size_of_group_1 + size_of_group_2]
494	        self.group_prefix_sum = [0]
495	        if self.meta.shapes is not None and self.meta.dtypes is not None:
496	            size_in_bytes = 0
497	            for shape, dtype in zip(self.meta.shapes, self.meta.dtypes, strict=True):
498	                size_in_bytes += shape.numel() * dtype.itemsize
499	                self.group_prefix_sum.append(size_in_bytes)
500	        else:
501	            self.group_prefix_sum.append(self.meta.get_size())
502	
503	    def __del__(self):
504	        """
505	        Destructor to ensure memory is released when the object is garbage collected.
506	        This acts as a safety net to prevent memory leaks if ref_count_down() is not
507	        called properly somewhere in the code path.
508	        """
509	        if self.parent_allocator is not None and self.is_valid():
510	            if self.meta.ref_count > 0 or self.meta.pin_count > 0:
511	                logger.warning(
512	                    "MemoryObj at %s is being garbage collected "
513	                    "with ref_count=%d, pin_count=%d. "
514	                    "This indicates ref_count_down()/unpin() was not called properly.",
515	                    self.meta.address,
516	                    self.meta.ref_count,
517	                    self.meta.pin_count,
518	                )
519	            self.parent_allocator.free(self)
520	
521	    def invalidate(self):
522	        self.valid = False
523	
524	    def is_valid(self):
525	        return self.valid
526	
527	    def get_size(self) -> int:
528	        return self.group_prefix_sum[-1]
529	
530	    # TODO(chunxiaozheng): use get_shapes and get_dtypes to replace
531	    #  get_shape and get_dtype
532	    def get_shape(self) -> torch.Size:
533	        return self.meta.shape
534	
535	    def get_dtype(self) -> torch.dtype:
536	        return self.meta.dtype
537	
538	    def get_shapes(self) -> list[torch.Size]:
539	        assert self.meta.shapes is not None
540	        return self.meta.shapes
541	
542	    def get_dtypes(self) -> list[torch.dtype]:
543	        assert self.meta.dtypes is not None
544	        return self.meta.dtypes
545	
546	    def get_memory_format(self) -> MemoryFormat:
547	        with self.lock:
548	            return self.meta.fmt
549	
550	    def get_physical_size(self) -> int:
551	        return self.meta.phy_size
552	
553	    def ref_count_up(self):
554	        with self.lock:
555	            self.meta.ref_count += 1
556	
557	    def ref_count_down(self):
558	        with self.lock:
559	            self.meta.ref_count -= 1
560	            if self.meta.ref_count < 0:
561	                logger.warning(
562	                    f"Ref count of MemoryObj {self.meta.address}"
563	                    f"is negative: {self.meta.ref_count}."
564	                    "Double free occurred somewhere."
565	                    "Setting ref count back to 0 as a hack but please find the bug."
566	                )
567	                self.meta.ref_count = 0
568	            if (
569	                self.meta.ref_count == 0
570	                and self.parent_allocator is not None
571	                and self.meta.pin_count == 0
572	            ):
573	                self.parent_allocator.free(self)
574	
575	    def get_ref_count(self) -> int:
576	        with self.lock:
577	            return self.meta.ref_count
578	
579	    def get_num_tokens(self) -> int:
580	        with self.lock:
581	            token_dim = self.meta.fmt.token_dim()
582	            return self.meta.shape[token_dim]
583	
584	    def pin(self) -> bool:
585	        with self.lock:
586	            # if pin_count is 0, indicates that the object is pinned for the first time
587	            if self.meta.pin_count == 0:
588	                TensorMemoryObj.monitor.update_pinned_memory_objs_count(1)
589	
590	            self.meta.pin_count += 1
591	
592	            # Register/update with PinMonitor for timeout tracking on every pin
593	            pin_monitor = PinMonitor.GetOrCreate()
594	            pin_monitor.on_pin(self)
595	            return True
596	
597	    def unpin(self) -> bool:
598	        with self.lock:
599	            self.meta.pin_count -= 1
600	
601	            # if pin_count is 0, indicates that the object is unpinned
602	            if self.meta.pin_count == 0:
603	                TensorMemoryObj.monitor.update_pinned_memory_objs_count(-1)
604	                # Unregister from PinMonitor when fully unpinned
605	                pin_monitor = PinMonitor.GetOrCreate()
606	                pin_monitor.on_unpin(self)
607	
608	            if self.meta.pin_count <= 0 and self.meta.ref_count <= 0:
609	                if self.parent_allocator is None:
610	                    logger.error(
611	                        "Parent allocator is None when trying to free MemoryObj."
612	                        "This could cause memory leak"
613	                    )
614	                else:
615	                    self.parent_allocator.free(self)
616	
617	            if self.meta.pin_count < 0:
618	                logger.warning(
619	                    f"Pin count of MemoryObj {self.meta.address}"
620	                    f"is negative: {self.meta.pin_count}."
621	                    "Double unpin occurred somewhere."
622	                    "Setting pin count back to 0 as a hack but please find the bug."
623	                )
624	                self.meta.pin_count = 0
625	            return True
626	
627	    @property
628	    def metadata(self) -> MemoryObjMetadata:
629	        with self.lock:
630	            return self.meta
631	
632	    @property
633	    def tensor(self) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
634	        if not self.valid:
635	            logger.warning("Trying to access an invalidated MemoryObj")
636	            return None
637	        assert self.meta.dtype is not None
638	        # TODO(Jiayi): consider caching the `get_size()`
639	        return (
640	            self.raw_data[: self.get_size()].view(self.meta.dtype).view(self.meta.shape)
641	        )
642	
643	    @property
644	    def byte_array(self) -> memoryview:
645	        # TODO: consider using one of the alternatives
646	
647	        # Alternative 1:
648	        # # PyTorch tensors support buffer protocol directly for CPU tensors
649	        # return memoryview(self.raw_data)
650	
651	        # Alternative 2:
652	        # assert self.raw_data.device.type == 'cpu',
653	        #   "byte_array only works with CPU tensors"
654	        # return memoryview(self.raw_data.contiguous().numpy())
655	
656	        num_bytes = self.raw_data.numel() * self.raw_data.element_size()
657	        ptr = self.raw_data.data_ptr()
658	        ubyte_ptr = ctypes.cast(ptr, ctypes.POINTER(ctypes.c_ubyte))
659	        byte_array = (ctypes.c_ubyte * num_bytes).from_address(
660	            ctypes.addressof(ubyte_ptr.contents)
661	        )
662	        return memoryview(byte_array)
663	
664	    @property
665	    def data_ptr(self) -> int:
666	        return self.raw_data.data_ptr()
667	
668	    @property
669	    def is_pinned(self) -> bool:
670	        return self.metadata.pin_count > 0
671	
672	    @property
673	    def can_evict(self) -> bool:
674	        """
675	        Check whether the memory obj can be evicted.
676	        A memory obj can be evicted if it is not pinned and ref_count=1.
677	        """
678	        return not self.is_pinned and self.get_ref_count() == 1
679	
680	    @property
681	    def raw_tensor(self) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
682	        if not self.valid:
683	            logger.warning("Trying to access an invalidated MemoryObj")
684	            return None
685	        return self.raw_data
686	
687	    def get_tensor(self, index: int) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
688	        if not self.valid:
689	            logger.warning("Trying to access an invalidated MemoryObj")
690	            return None
691	        assert self.meta.shapes is not None
692	        assert self.meta.dtypes is not None
693	        begin = self.group_prefix_sum[index]
694	        end = self.group_prefix_sum[index + 1]
695	        return (
696	            self.raw_data[begin:end]
697	            .view(self.meta.dtypes[index])
698	            .view(self.meta.shapes[index])
699	        )

62	# Helper function to allocate and copy memory objects between D and H
63	def allocate_and_copy_objects(
64	    allocator_backend: AllocatorBackendInterface,
65	    keys: Sequence[CacheEngineKey],
66	    src_memory_objs: list[MemoryObj],
67	    stream: torch.cuda.Stream,
68	) -> tuple[Sequence[CacheEngineKey], list[MemoryObj]]:
69	    """
70	    Allocate the memory objects and copy the data from src_memory_objs to
71	    the newly allocated memory objects
72	
73	    Args:
74	        allocator_backend: the allocator backend to allocate the new memory
75	          objects
76	        keys: the cache engine keys corresponding to the memory objects
77	        src_memory_objs: the memory objects to copy from
78	        stream: the cuda stream to run the copy in
79	
80	    Returns:
81	        - list of cache engine keys that corresponds to the memory objects
82	          that has been successfully allocated
83	        - list of the memory objects that has been successfully allocated
84	    """
85	    allocated_objects = []
86	    for key, src_memory_obj in zip(keys, src_memory_objs, strict=False):
87	        if allocator_backend.contains(key):
88	            continue
89	        memory_obj = allocator_backend.allocate(
90	            src_memory_obj.get_shape(),
91	            src_memory_obj.get_dtype(),
92	            fmt=src_memory_obj.meta.fmt,
93	            eviction=True,
94	            busy_loop=False,
95	        )
96	
97	        if memory_obj is None:
98	            break
99	
100	        if memory_obj.tensor is None:
101	            # This should not happen with current implementation,
102	            # but handle it defensively to avoid memory leak
103	            logger.warning(
104	                "Allocated MemoryObj has None tensor, this is unexpected. "
105	                "Releasing the memory object."
106	            )
107	            memory_obj.ref_count_down()
108	            break
109	
110	        with torch.cuda.stream(stream):
111	            memory_obj.tensor.copy_(src_memory_obj.tensor, non_blocking=True)
112	        allocated_objects.append(memory_obj)
113	
114	    if stream is not None:
115	        stream.synchronize()
116	    return keys[: len(allocated_objects)], allocated_objects

119	class WeightedSemaphore:
120	    def __init__(self, chunk_budget: int):
121	        # it is physically impossible to have more fragmentation than 50%
122	        # when all of the chunks are of the same size (save_unfull_chunk=False)
123	        # so we can safely allocate half of the chunk budget for concurrent requests
124	        self._concurrent_budget_cap = chunk_budget // 2
125	        self._chunk_budget_cap = chunk_budget
126	        self._current_chunks = self._concurrent_budget_cap
127	        self._cond = asyncio.Condition()
128	
129	    async def acquire(self, n: int = 1) -> None:
130	        if n > self._chunk_budget_cap:
131	            raise ValueError(
132	                f"Trying to acquire {n} chunks, "
133	                f"Cannot acquire more than {self._chunk_budget_cap} chunks"
134	                "Please set the max local cpu size to a larger value"
135	            )
136	
137	        async with self._cond:
138	            logger.debug(f"WeightedSemaphore: Attempting to acquire {n} chunks")
139	            if n <= self._concurrent_budget_cap:
140	                await self._cond.wait_for(lambda: self._current_chunks >= n)
141	                self._current_chunks -= n
142	            else:
143	                # Oversized case: require exclusive access
144	                await self._cond.wait_for(
145	                    lambda: self._current_chunks == self._concurrent_budget_cap
146	                )
147	                # Reserve everything
148	                self._current_chunks = 0
149	            logger.debug(
150	                f"WeightedSemaphore: Acquired {n} chunks, "
151	                f"remaining chunks: {self._current_chunks}"
152	            )
153	
154	    async def release(self, n: int = 1) -> None:
155	        async with self._cond:
156	            if n <= self._concurrent_budget_cap:
157	                self._current_chunks += n
158	            else:
159	                self._current_chunks = self._concurrent_budget_cap
160	            self._cond.notify_all()
161

163	class AsyncMultiSerializer:
164	    """
165	    Prevent race conditions where multiple batched_get's cause the local CPU
166	    backend to allocate memory objects in parallel and get deadlocked.
167	    Make the assumption that the save_unfull_chunk is False so that we
168	    can assume that we can always use 50% of the given memory
169	    """
170	
171	    def __init__(
172	        self,
173	        allocator_backend: AllocatorBackendInterface,
174	        loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop,
175	    ):
176	        self.chunk_budget = allocator_backend.calculate_chunk_budget()
177	        self._sem = WeightedSemaphore(self.chunk_budget)
178	        self.loop = loop
179	
180	    async def run(
181	        self,
182	        coro_fn: Coroutine[Any, Any, Any],
183	        num_chunks: int,
184	    ) -> Any:
185	        await self._sem.acquire(num_chunks)
186	        try:
187	            return await coro_fn
188	        finally:
189	            await self._sem.release(num_chunks)
190

192	class AsyncSingleSerializer:
193	    """
194	    Prevent race conditions in a naive way by forcing each request that
195	    is passed through to be serialized
196	    """
197	
198	    def __init__(self, loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop):
199	        self.loop = loop
200	        # lazy init in run
201	        self.lock: Optional[asyncio.Lock] = None
202	
203	    async def run(self, coro_fn: Coroutine[Any, Any, Any], *args, **kwargs) -> Any:
204	        # we need to lazily initialize the lock to
205	        # place it on the calling event loop
206	        if self.lock is None:
207	            self.lock = asyncio.Lock()
208	        async with self.lock:  # type: ignore
209	            return await coro_fn
210	
211	
212	AsyncSerializer = Union[AsyncSingleSerializer, AsyncMultiSerializer]
